title: About Taiwanese Mahjong
content: Taiwanese Mahjong is a fast-paced, social tile game played by four players that combines strategy, memory, and a bit of luck. Each player starts with 16 tiles and takes turns drawing and discarding to form a winning hand made of sets and a pair.

title: Tile Types Overview
content: There are 6 types of tiles in Taiwanese Mahjong:
details: Characters — 9 tiles (1-9), x4 each = 36 total (represent ten thousands)
Winds — 4 tiles (East/South/West/North), x4 each = 16 total. Cannot form Chows — only Pongs and Kongs.
Dragons — 3 tiles (Red/Green/White), x4 each = 12 total. Cannot form Chows — only Pongs and Kongs.
Flowers & Seasons — 4 Flower + 4 Season tiles, each appears once = 8 total. When drawn, reveal immediately and draw a replacement.

title: Forming Melds
content: Melds are the sets you need to build a winning hand. There are 4 types:
details: Pong — A set of 3 identical tiles (triplet). Most common way to form a meld.
Kong — A set of 4 identical tiles. Largest possible meld of identical tiles.
Chow — A sequence of 3 consecutive tiles of the same suit (e.g., 4-5-6 Bamboo). Numbers must be in order. You can only Chow from the player directly ahead of you.
Eyes — A pair of 2 identical tiles. Every winning hand needs one pair.

title: How to Win (Hu)
content: A basic winning hand consists of 5 melds (Pong, Kong, or Chow) plus 1 pair of identical tiles (Eyes). This is called 'Hu'.
details: You need exactly 5 melds + 1 pair to win.
Each meld can be any combination of Pongs, Kongs, or Chows.
If you are one tile away from completing your winning hand, you are in a 'Ready Hand'.

title: Scoring: Bonus Points (Tai)
content: In Taiwanese Mahjong, winning hands earn bonus points called Tai. The more Tai you have, the more you win!
details: All Triplets (+4 Tai) — A winning hand made entirely of Pongs and Kongs, no Chows allowed.
Full Flush (+4 Tai) — A winning hand using only one suit (Bamboo, Circle, or Character), no Honor tiles.
Concealed Hand (+1 Tai) — You win without claiming any discarded tiles. All melds formed by drawing tiles yourself.
Self-Draw (+1 Tai) — You draw the winning tile yourself from the wall instead of claiming a discard. All three other players pay you!

title: Review: Tiles & Melds
content: Let's review what you've learned before the practice section.

sections.heading: Tile Types
points: Bamboo, Circles, Characters — 9 each, x4 = 36 per suit
Winds (E/S/W/N) — x4 each = 16. No Chows, only Pongs & Kongs.
Dragons (Red/Green/White) — x4 each = 12. No Chows, only Pongs & Kongs.
Flowers/Seasons — 8 total, each x1. Reveal & draw replacement.

sections.heading: Melds
points: Chow = 3 consecutive tiles, same suit (only from player ahead)

sections.heading: Winning & Scoring
points: Ready Hand = one tile away from winning
All Triplets = +4 Tai | Full Flush = +4 Tai
